name: protocol-guardrails
description: Harness-enforced guardrails (hooks + skills) + minimal doc steering so build-os agents route through /plan → /approve → /ship instead of ad-hoc building on main.
globalRules: Sequential execution only — no parallel subagents on the same codebase.
Fail-open is mandatory for anything in the global hook path (exit 0 on any ambiguity/error).
Hook command paths in settings.json must be ABSOLUTE: /Users/johnkoht/.claude/build/hooks/... (no ~).
Dark-code audit: every new skill/hook must be invocable/wired, not just present in the repo.
~/.claude/commands is a materialized real dir — new/modified skills need `build-config sync` to go live.

tasks.title: Edit-guard PreToolUse hook
tasks.description: Create build/hooks/isolation-guard.sh — a PreToolUse hook that fires on Edit|Write|MultiEdit|NotebookEdit and, in build-os-managed projects only, surfaces an `ask` prompt when the agent tries to edit SOURCE in the MAIN checkout, routing it to /ship or /hotfix. This is the backstop that catches ad-hoc builds on main.
acceptanceCriteria: Script at build/hooks/isolation-guard.sh, executable, reads tool-call JSON on stdin.
FAIL-OPEN: exit 0 on any ambiguity — no git repo, missing file_path, parse failure, unknown state.
Fast early-exit BEFORE expensive work when repo is not a git repo OR has no .build/ marker at toplevel.
Fires only when: file is in the MAIN checkout (git rev-parse --git-dir contains no '/worktrees/'), repo toplevel has a .build/ dir, and the path is NOT allowlisted.
Allowlist by DIRECTORY prefix only (plans/, memory/, .build/, LEARNINGS.md) — never by file extension (build-os source is markdown).
Resolve tool_input.file_path against stdin `cwd` when relative BEFORE prefix-matching (review note 5).

On block: emit {"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"ask","permissionDecisionReason":"..."}}. Reason routes to /ship (3+ steps) or /hotfix; must NOT say 'just create a worktree' (avoids false-compliance bypass).
Uses jq for JSON parsing; if jq absent, fail-open (exit 0).

Unit test / test harness: (a) edit .ts in a NON-build repo → passes through; (b) source edit on main of a .build repo → ask; (c) plans/ edit on main of a .build repo → passes; (d) edit inside a worktree → passes.

tasks.title: Plan-mode redirect hook (+ empirical hookability test)
tasks.description: Determine empirically whether EnterPlanMode is PreToolUse-matchable, then add a hook that denies native plan mode in .build/ projects and redirects the agent to /plan. If not hookable, fall back to a CLAUDE.md callout (handled in task-7) and document the gap.
acceptanceCriteria: STEP 1 (before writing hook code): empirically verify a PreToolUse matcher on `EnterPlanMode` actually fires — install a trivial logging matcher, trigger plan mode, confirm invocation. Record the result in the task report and build-log.

If hookable: build/hooks/plan-redirect.sh — PreToolUse matcher `EnterPlanMode`, self-gated to .build/ repos, emits permissionDecision `deny` with reason redirecting to /plan (saves to plans/, gates with /approve). Fail-open.
If NOT hookable: skip the hook, note the residual gap; task-7's CLAUDE.md callout is the fallback.
Self-gating identical to task-1 (only .build/ repos); non-build repos keep native plan mode.
Does not affect the user's manual plan-mode toggle where possible (document observed behavior).

tasks.title: /plan skill
tasks.description: Create commands/plan.md — our plan-mode replacement. A normal (non-read-only) skill, model- AND user-invocable, that explores read-only first, discusses trade-offs, then writes plans/{slug}/plan.md (status: draft) and ends each round with a keep-editing / /approve prompt. Never auto-executes. Ends the saved plan with the pre-commitment line.
filesToRead: build/AGENTS.md ([Project Structure] plan layout, [Skills] frontmatter conventions)
acceptanceCriteria: commands/plan.md with frontmatter: name: plan, description, bucket: planning. Model+user invocable (NO disable-model-invocation).

Flow: (1) read-only explore + discuss trade-offs; (2) write plans/{slug}/plan.md with status: draft and standard frontmatter (title, slug, status, created, has_pre_mortem/has_review/has_prd: false); (3) end each iteration with the literal two options: '1. keep editing  2. /approve'.
Explicitly instructs: do NOT implement, do NOT enter native plan mode, do NOT auto-execute after presenting the plan.
The saved plan.md ends with the pre-commitment line: 'On approval → /approve → /ship {slug}'.
Slug derivation matches existing convention (kebab-case of title).

tasks.title: /approve skill
tasks.description: Create commands/approve.md — a user-only gate that marks the pending plan approved and prints the next step. disable-model-invocation: true so Claude can never auto-invoke it.
acceptanceCriteria: commands/approve.md with frontmatter including `disable-model-invocation: true` (user-only).
Sets plans/{slug}/plan.md frontmatter status: approved (from draft/planned).
If the plan exists only in conversation, saves it to plans/{slug}/plan.md first, then approves.
Prints the next-step menu: /ship (full) | /ship lite (3-5 step low-risk) | /build (have PRD+worktree).
Does nothing destructive; idempotent if already approved.

tasks.title: Strengthen /hotfix (mandatory reviewer + tests)
tasks.description: Update commands/hotfix.md so the reviewer subagent is MANDATORY (not a self-review fallback) and tests are always run. This makes /hotfix the trustworthy target for routed fix requests.
filesToRead: commands/hotfix.md (current Phase 3 review is optional fallback)
acceptanceCriteria: Phase 3 reviewer subagent is required; the 'if subagent not available, self-review' escape is removed or downgraded to an explicit last resort with a warning.
Phase 2 explicitly requires running quality gates + the regression test before review.
No change to /hotfix's single-bug scope; keep it lighter than a PRD.

tasks.title: ship.md overhaul: /ship lite + native worktrees + fix-routing
tasks.description: Single task owning ALL ship.md edits to avoid conflicts: add a /ship lite mode; replace hand-rolled `git worktree add`/`git worktree remove` with native EnterWorktree/ExitWorktree (worktrees land in .claude/worktrees/); add a fix-requests → /hotfix routing note; add .claude/worktrees/ to .gitignore; set worktree.baseRef: head.
filesToRead: commands/ship.md (Phases 3 and 6, Pre-Flight gate, When to Use)
acceptanceCriteria: /ship lite documented in 'When to Use' + a Pre-Flight condition: lite bypasses Phase 1.2 (pre-mortem) and 1.3 (review) even when frontmatter flags are false, keeps Phases 2-6 (PRD, worktree, build, wrap, merge). Specify the exact invocation (e.g. `/ship lite {slug}`) and how pre-flight detects it (review note 7).
Phase 3 uses native EnterWorktree (worktrees in .claude/worktrees/); Phase 6 uses ExitWorktree (remove). Remove the hand-rolled `git worktree add ../{repo}-worktrees/...`.
Note that EnterWorktree needs worktree.baseRef: head so the worktree includes the just-committed plan/PRD artifacts — reference the settings key (set in task-8).
Keep the existing Worktree Guard (git-dir check) — add a one-line note that it passes correctly from .claude/worktrees/ paths (review note 3).
Add a routing note in the build/fix phases: 'Fix requests during or after execution → /hotfix, never ad-hoc patches.'
.gitignore gains a `.claude/worktrees/` entry (review note 4).

tasks.title: Doc steer + routing (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, build.md)
tasks.description: Minimal, corrective doc changes. Point plan_first at /plan; fix isolation_gate to guard code-writing-on-main (not branch-switching); add a small_tasks boundary; soften zero_context_switching; add a CLAUDE.md callout to use /plan (also the fallback if task-2's hook isn't possible); add a fix-routing note to build.md.
filesToRead: build/AGENTS.md (lines 11 small_tasks, 22 isolation_gate; plan_first is in CLAUDE.md)
acceptanceCriteria: AGENTS.md isolation_gate rewritten to prohibit editing SOURCE in the main checkout (not just branch-switching); names the failure mode (ad-hoc build after approval); says a hand-made worktree is not a substitute for /ship.
AGENTS.md small_tasks gains an explicit boundary: act directly only for <=2 files, no new files, no dep/schema changes, and no written plan; else route to /plan → /ship (or /hotfix for bugs).
plan_first (CLAUDE.md) points to /plan and steers away from native plan mode.
zero_context_switching softened to apply to bugs; features route through the flow.
CLAUDE.md gains a short callout: after a plan is approved, run /approve then /ship; use /plan not native plan mode. Kept minimal (steer, don't bloat).
build.md gains a 'fix requests → /hotfix, never ad-hoc' note.
Net additions are minimal and corrective — no walls of text.

tasks.title: install.sh + settings.json merge + sync + retrofit wiring
tasks.description: Wire everything so it takes effect: idempotent hooks merge into ~/.claude/settings.json (absolute paths, JSON-validated, backup first), trigger build-config sync so new/modified skills go live, and make new-project + build-os-retrofit ensure .build/ + hooks install + .claude/worktrees/ gitignore. Also set worktree.baseRef: head.
filesToRead: install.sh (symlink model; errors on real commands dir at lines 13-15)
plans/protocol-guardrails/pre-mortem.md (R2 — do NOT corrupt live settings.json)
acceptanceCriteria: install.sh gains an idempotent settings.json hooks-merge step: back up settings.json, add PreToolUse hook(s) with ABSOLUTE command path, validate the result parses as JSON, no-op on re-run. R2: develop/test the merge against a COPY; the live merge is a deliberate, validated step.
install.sh sets worktree.baseRef: head in settings.json (idempotent).

install.sh (or its output) runs / instructs `build-config sync` so /plan, /approve, and modified ship/hotfix/build reach the materialized ~/.claude/commands. DARK-CODE AC: /plan and /approve are invocable as slash commands after sync.
new-project ensures the new project has .build/ and .claude/worktrees/ gitignored, and that hooks are installed.
build-os-retrofit adds .build/ to existing projects (so the guard protects them) and installs/verifies hooks.
build-os itself is intentionally NOT given .build/ (review note 6 — deferred); document this.

tasks.title: Backlog file
tasks.description: Create plans/protocol-guardrails/backlog.md capturing all deferred items so nothing is lost.
acceptanceCriteria: backlog.md lists: SessionStart injection, UserPromptSubmit approval nudge, .build/.planning read-only sentinel, statusline MAIN/worktree indicator, edit-guard ask→deny escalation, separate /fix skill, retrofit build-os itself (with the trade-off noted).
Each item has a one-line rationale / trigger for when to pull it off the backlog.
